polymarket_events_sample.title: Will Israel launch a major ground offensive in Lebanon by March 31?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a major ground offensive in Lebanon by March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "major ground offensive" is defined as a large-scale military operation that involves more than 1,000 Israeli ground forces entering Lebanese territory that was not under Israeli control at the start of the offensive. This excludes smaller raids, special operations, or limited incursions directly bordering buffer zones already under Israeli control.
 
The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on a major Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon by March 31 stands at near 100% "Yes," propelled by Prime Minister Netanyahu's October 2024 authorization of expanded IDF operations into southern Lebanon targeting Hezbollah strongholds, following the group's intensified rocket barrages and the assassination of leader Hassan Nasrallah. Recent developments include ongoing limited ground raids, evacuation orders for Lebanese border villages, and official statements signaling preparations for broader incursions to secure the frontier. Stalled U.S.-brokered ceasefire talks and Hezbollah's refusal to withdraw north of the Litani River reinforce this trajectory. Realistic off-ramps remain slim but include sudden diplomatic breakthroughs or de-escalation signals from Tehran, though historical precedents favor Israeli action amid persistent threats.

polymarket_events_sample.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's Euro 2024 triumph over England has propelled them to the top of 2026 FIFA World Cup winner markets at 15.4%, with traders betting on their golden generation including Lamine Yamal and Pedri entering peak form. Yet the race stays neck-and-neck, as England's runner-up resilience (12.8%), France's Mbappé-led firepower (10.7%), and Argentina's defending champion aura under aging Messi (10.1%) keep probabilities clustered. Brazil's rebuilding depth (8.6%) and Norway's Haaland threat (3.3%) add volatility, amplified by the expanded 48-team format, grueling qualifiers, and two years of potential injuries or momentum shifts across UEFA and CONMEBOL powerhouses. Home-host USA ranks low at 1.7%, highlighting Europe's South America's talent edge.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit__ by end of March?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probabilities to Crude Oil (CL) hitting the target by March's end, primarily driven by persistent US inventory builds and tepid Chinese demand growth amid economic slowdown. WTI futures currently trade around $78/bbl, pressured lower after last week's EIA report revealed a 3.2 million barrel crude stockpile increase, exceeding forecasts. OPEC+ production cuts offer downside protection near $70, but recession risks from sticky inflation and delayed Fed rate cuts limit upside potential. Critical catalysts ahead include tomorrow's EIA inventory data and the March 20 FOMC meeting, where dot plot revisions could reshape growth outlooks and fuel consumption forecasts, underscoring market uncertainty.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
polymarket_events_sample.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: JD Vance's lead at 18.6% on Polymarket reflects trader consensus on his VP role under President-elect Trump, positioning him as the MAGA heir apparent amid term limits barring Trump's 2028 run. Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.4%, buoyed by his national profile as California governor and Democratic recalibration post-Harris's defeat. Marco Rubio's 11.5% share rises with his Secretary of State nomination, signaling executive gravitas. The pack remains tight due to the three-year horizon before primaries, fragmented party fields, and uncertain midterm dynamics in 2026, which could widen gaps via legislative wins, scandals, or endorsements.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 24.4% for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination in a fragmented field following the 2024 election, driven by his executive record as California governor, national media profile from policy debates, and strong fundraising network. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.2%, buoyed by progressive base enthusiasm and social media reach, while Jon Ossoff's 6.3% reflects his Georgia Senate incumbency and swing-state appeal. Differentiators include governing experience versus legislative profiles, centrist pragmatism against ideological purity, and electability in battlegrounds. Support consolidation may hinge on 2026 midterm outcomes, early donor commitments, or endorsements from Biden-era figures, underscoring traders' focus on leadership viability amid uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sacramento Kings' slim 51% implied probability against the Charlotte Hornets stems from their stronger roster depth with De'Aaron Fox and Domantas Sabonis driving efficient offense, offset by Charlotte's home-court edge at Spectrum Center and LaMelo Ball's dynamic playmaking in recent outings. Trader sentiment balances Kings' 48.6% win rate on the road against Hornets' improved defense ranking 15th over last five games, amid mutual rest advantages post-back-to-backs. Key swing factors include Fox's questionable ankle (per official report)—confirmation of full participation could lift Kings to 58%+, while Hornets' Brandon Miller exceeding 25 points shifts momentum toward Charlotte upset, as seen in their 3-2 home underdog record this season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment prices the Nuggets at a slim 50.5% implied probability for their Western Conference showdown with the Suns, reflecting a fiercely competitive balance between Denver's reigning champion core—led by Nikola Jokic's unmatched playmaking—and Phoenix's elite scoring duo of Kevin Durant and Devin Booker. Recent developments include Jamal Murray shaking off a hamstring tweak to join the probable starters per the Nuggets' injury report, bolstering their backcourt edge, while Bradley Beal remains sidelined for the Suns with a calf strain, thinning their depth. Momentum favors Denver off two straight road wins, but Phoenix's home-court rest advantage and top-5 defense could shift odds if Durant exploits mismatches; pre-game lineup confirmations will be pivotal.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Orleans' crippling injury wave—Zion Williamson out with a hamstring strain, Brandon Ingram sidelined by ankle issues, and CJ McCollum questionable with a quad—has decimated the Pelicans' offense, fueling the Knicks' towering 82.5% implied probability. New York enters healthy and surging, with Jalen Brunson dropping 30+ points routinely amid a four-game win streak and elite home defense at Madison Square Garden (8-2 lately). Pelicans limp in on a three-game skid, outscored by 12 per 100 possessions without their stars, amplifying Knicks' edge in pace control and rebounding battles that traders are pricing as near-certain dominance.
